notice: Maintenance & failover truth: every notice declares whether it is a maintenance, drain, failover, or tenant-migration window, the exact window time / timezone / offset, the affected deployment scope and write classes, which queued publish-later or local-draft work survives (kept separate from successful hosted mutations), what stays local-safe, and any changed tenant / region / endpoint boundary. A notice reads as current only while it is active and its last refresh is current — otherwise it downgrades and names why, and never collapses into a generic degraded banner. Shell, activity history, CLI / headless inspect, diagnostics, and support exports read this record verbatim.
title: Sync maintenance window (rescheduled)
summary: This maintenance window was rescheduled; a newer notice replaces it.

blocked_writes.note: Comments retry after the rescheduled window; see the replacement notice.

local_continuity.continuity_summary: Local work continues; this notice is superseded by a rescheduled one.

display_copy.primary_status_line: Scheduled maintenance window: This maintenance window was rescheduled; a newer notice replaces it.
display_copy.queued_preserved_line: 0 intents preserved (0 publish-later queued, 0 local drafts); they survive the window.
display_copy.succeeded_line: 0 hosted mutations already landed and are listed separately from queued work.
display_copy.local_continuity_line: Local work continues; this notice is superseded by a rescheduled one.
display_copy.boundary_change_line: No tenant / region / endpoint boundary change.
display_copy.freshness_line: This notice is Superseded — newer notice exists — Superseded by a newer notice.
display_copy.follow_up_line: Continue local work; managed writes resume when the window ends.
display_copy.stale_label: This continuity notice is Superseded — newer notice exists — do not read it as current operational truth; re-check live service health.
